Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.box2d

Interface FlixelBox2DObject

public interface FlixelBox2DObject
Opt-in interface for integrating libGDX Box2D physics with any FlixelObject subclass. Implementors get a managed Body whose position and angle are automatically synced back to the object's x, y, and angle each frame (via syncFromBody()).

Usage


 public class Crate extends FlixelSprite implements FlixelBox2DObject {
   private Body body;

   public Body getBody() { return body; }
   public void setBody(Body body) { this.body = body; }

   public void create() {
     loadGraphic(...);
     createRectangularBody(Flixel.getWorld());
   }
 }

Pixel-to-Meter Scale

All default helpers use FlixelConstants.Physics.PIXELS_PER_METER (100 px = 1 m). Override individual methods if your game uses a different scale.

Motion

When a Box2D body is present, the implementor should not rely on FlixelObject.updateMotion(float) for movement; instead the World step drives the body and syncFromBody() copies the result back to the object. If you still set moves = true alongside a body, the custom integration will run after the body sync and may produce unexpected results.
See Also:
  • libGDX Box2D

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default void
    applyForce(float forceX, float forceY)
    Applies a force at the body's center of mass.
    default void
    applyLinearImpulse(float impulseX, float impulseY)
    Applies an impulse at the body's center of mass.
    default void
    applyVelocityToBody()
    Applies the object's current velocityX / velocityY to the body as a linear velocity (converted from px/s to m/s).
    default com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.Body
    createRectangularBody(com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.World world)
    Creates a dynamic rectangular body from the object's current x, y, width, and height.
    default com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.Body
    createRectangularBody(com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.World world, com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.BodyDef bodyDef)
    Creates a rectangular body with a custom BodyDef from the object's current dimensions.
    default com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.Body
    createRectangularBody(com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.World world, com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.BodyDef bodyDef, com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.FixtureDef fixtureDef)
    Creates a rectangular body with full control over body and fixture definitions.
    default void
    destroyBody(com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.World world)
    Destroys the body in the given world and sets the internal reference to null.
    com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.Body
    getBody()
    Returns the Box2D body attached to this object, or null if no body has been created yet.
    void
    setBody(com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.Body body)
    Stores a reference to the given Box2D body.
    default void
    syncFromBody()
    Copies the Box2D body's position and angle back to the FlixelObject's x, y, and angle.
    default void
    syncToBody()
    Copies the FlixelObject's current x, y, and angle to the Box2D body.

  • Method Details

    • getBody

      com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.Body getBody()
      Returns the Box2D body attached to this object, or null if no body has been created yet.

    • setBody

      void setBody(com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.Body body)
      Stores a reference to the given Box2D body. Called by the default createRectangularBody helpers; implementors must provide the backing field.

    • createRectangularBody

      default com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.Body createRectangularBody(com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.World world)
      Creates a dynamic rectangular body from the object's current x, y, width, and height.
      Parameters:
      world - The Box2D world to create the body in.
      Returns:
      The created body, or null if this is not a FlixelObject.

    • createRectangularBody

      default com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.Body createRectangularBody(com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.World world, com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.BodyDef bodyDef)
      Creates a rectangular body with a custom BodyDef from the object's current dimensions. A default box PolygonShape and FixtureDef (density = 1, friction = 0.3, restitution = 0) are applied. Override createRectangularBody(World, BodyDef, FixtureDef) for full control.
      Parameters:
      world - The Box2D world.
      bodyDef - The body definition to use.
      Returns:
      The created body, or null if this is not a FlixelObject.

    • createRectangularBody

      default com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.Body createRectangularBody(com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.World world, com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.BodyDef bodyDef, com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.FixtureDef fixtureDef)
      Creates a rectangular body with full control over body and fixture definitions. The body is positioned at the center of the object's bounding box (converted to meters) and a box shape is created from the object's width and height.
      Parameters:
      world - The Box2D world.
      bodyDef - The body definition.
      fixtureDef - The fixture definition. The shape will be set automatically.
      Returns:
      The created body, or null if this is not a FlixelObject.

    • syncFromBody

      default void syncFromBody()
      Copies the Box2D body's position and angle back to the FlixelObject's x, y, and angle. Called automatically by the engine after each world step for every active FlixelBox2DObject.

      Box2D positions represent the body center; this method converts to the top-left convention used by FlixelObject.

    • syncToBody

      default void syncToBody()
      Copies the FlixelObject's current x, y, and angle to the Box2D body. Useful after teleporting an object.

    • applyVelocityToBody

      default void applyVelocityToBody()
      Applies the object's current velocityX / velocityY to the body as a linear velocity (converted from px/s to m/s).

    • applyLinearImpulse

      default void applyLinearImpulse(float impulseX, float impulseY)
      Applies an impulse at the body's center of mass.
      Parameters:
      impulseX - Horizontal impulse in Newton-seconds.
      impulseY - Vertical impulse in Newton-seconds.

    • applyForce

      default void applyForce(float forceX, float forceY)
      Applies a force at the body's center of mass.
      Parameters:
      forceX - Horizontal force in Newtons.
      forceY - Vertical force in Newtons.

    • destroyBody

      default void destroyBody(com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.World world)
      Destroys the body in the given world and sets the internal reference to null. Safe to call when the body is already null.
      Parameters:
      world - The Box2D world the body belongs to.
